---
title: "Marion County, Oregon Solid Waste Rates Review"
year: 2025
image: /images/marion-or.jpg
image-alt: "Image of Marion County, Oregon"
categories: ["Rate Setting"]
---
{{< include _return-to-pf.qmd >}}

::: {.article}
Marion County regulates eight companies that provide waste and recycling to the
unincorporated areas of the county. The process requires franchised companies
to submit annual detailed cost reports, which are reviewed, adjusted, and
compiled by collection areas—urban or rural. Waste was disposed of at the
Waste-to-Energy facility until January 2025; however, changes to Oregon laws
made incineration too expensive, requiring Covanta to cancel its contract with
Marion County. 

The shutdown of the Covanta facility requires the County to undergo a transfer
facility planning process to determine the best long-term disposal option(s).
Mr. Bell is currently a subcontractor on this planning project.


[Meeting agenda](https://www.co.marion.or.us/BOC/Documents/2015BoardCalendars/PW_Pub%20Hear_Solid%20Waste%20Collection%20Rates.pdf)
:::
